perturbation.original_text: The Offer shall be open for acceptance from the time of commencement until a time that is not earlier than 3:30 p.m. (Tokyo time) on the 30t h Japan Business Day from and including the Launch Date (as adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f) below, the "Offer Period").
perturbation.changed_text: The Offer shall be open for acceptance from the time of commencement until a time that is not earlier than 3:30 p.m. (Tokyo time) on the 30t h Japan Business Day from and including the Launch Date (as adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f) below, the "Offer Period"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the offer period shall automatically expire 20 Japan Business Days from the Launch Date, regardless of any adjustments.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying two different end dates for the offer period. The original text states it ends on the 30th Japan Business Day from the Launch Date (with adjustments), while the added sentence stipulates it automatically expires after 20 Japan Business Days, regardless of adjustments, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the offer period. This creates an in-text contradiction in the same section where the conflicting deadline is now defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Commencement of the Offer. Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Acquiror agrees to commence the Offer on August 27, 2015 (the "Launch Date") to acquire for cash (i) all of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock at the Share Offer Price and (ii) all of the outstanding Stock Options at the price as set out in Schedule I. The Offer shall be open for acceptance from the time of commencement until a time that is not earlier than 3:30 p.m. (Tokyo time) on the 30t h Japan Business Day from and including the Launch Date (as adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f) below, the "Offer Period"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the offer period shall automatically expire 20 Japan Business Days from the Launch Date, regardless of any adjustments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction by specifying two different expiry dates for the offer period. The original text states the offer period ends on the 30th Japan Business Day, whereas the changed text stipulates that it ends on the 20th Japan Business Day, regardless of any adjustments. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, where the offer period is defined with conflicting deadlines, leading to a strong contradiction. This directly conflicts with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections (in this case, parts of the same section) of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: Settlement Date" shall mean the 5t h Japan Business Day following the last day of the Offer Period, except as such date may be adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f).
perturbation.changed_text: "Settlement Date" shall mean the 5t h Japan Business Day following the last day of the Offer Period, except as such date may be adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Settlement Date will be no later than 3 Japan Business Days following the end of the Offer Period and cannot be adjusted.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying two different constraints for the settlement date. The original text sets the Settlement Date as the 5th Japan Business Day following the end of the Offer Period, subject to adjustments per Section 2.02(f). The added sentence mandates it must be no later than 3 Japan Business Days following the Offer Period's end, and is not adjustable, therefore conflicting with the original definition. This creates an in-text contradiction in the same section where the conflicting definition is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Settlement Date" shall mean the 5t h Japan Business Day following the last day of the Offer Period, except as such date may be adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Settlement Date will be no later than 3 Japan Business Days following the end of the Offer Period and cannot be adjusted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within the same definition. The original definition of 'Settlement Date' allows for adjustments according to Section 2.02(f), setting it as the 5th Japan Business Day after the Offer Period. The added sentence overrides this by mandating that the Settlement Date cannot be later than the 3rd Japan Business Day and cannot be adjusted. Since the changed text now contains conflicting information about how the settlement date is defined, it introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
